title: Rotate immediately on any exposure
description: Treat any secret that reached source, history, logs, or an untrusted party as compromised and rotate it before doing anything else.
body: Use when a secret is found committed, logged, or shared. Do not assume a secret is safe because it was only briefly exposed or buried in history. Actions: revoke the exposed credential at its issuer; issue a replacement; update the secret manager and consumers; then scrub the exposure. Validation signals include the old credential being rejected by the issuing system and services running on the new one. Risk boundary: deleting the file without rotating leaves a live compromised key, so rotation is mandatory and removal alone is never sufficient; escalate exposure to a human owner.
